nodes.speaker: Alien7
lines: You. Yes, you, the faller. You fly, don't you? Everyone says you fly.

There's a base on the moon. MoonBase — we were not an imaginative generation. Twelve years ago the supply runs stopped, nobody ever told ME why, and the keeper up there still owes me for six crates of preserves.

responses.buttonText: You want me to collect a twelve-year-old debt?

nodes.speaker: Alien7
lines: Orders. From whom? 'The Accord.' Which part of the Accord? 'The Accord.'
Twelve years of that answer. I sell goods, faller — I know the sound of a price nobody wants to say out loud.

nodes.speaker: Alien7
lines: I want you to carry ONE crate up, knock on the door, and come back able to look me in the eye and tell me what you saw.
The crate is a formality. The looking is the job.

nodes.speaker: Alien7
lines: The crate's beside the stall. Mind your landing speed — that base glass is older than my complaints, and my complaints are historic.

nodes.speaker: Alien7
lines: It'll keep. Debts and moons, faller — neither one goes anywhere.
